children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Subcommittee shall coordinate Federal activities on ocean and coastal acidification and establish and maintain an interagency working group.

children.heading: Membership
children.label: 2 Membership
children.text: The interagency working group on ocean acidification shall be comprised of senior representatives from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, the National Science Foundation, the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, the United States Geological Survey, the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, the Environmental Protection Agency, the Department of Agriculture, the Department of State, the Department of Energy, the Department of the Navy, the National Park Service, the Bureau of Indian Affairs, the National Institute of Standards and Technology, the Smithsonian Institution, and such other Federal agencies as appropriate.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: 3 Chair
children.text: The interagency working group shall be chaired by the representative from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.
develop the strategic research and monitoring plan to guide Federal research on ocean acidification required under and oversee the implementation of the plan;section 3704 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the potential impacts of ocean acidification and coastal acidification on marine organisms and marine ecosystems; and

children.label: B
children.text: adaptation and mitigation strategies to conserve marine organisms and ecosystems exposed to ocean acidification and coastal acidification;
facilitate communication and outreach opportunities with nongovernmental organizations and members of the stakeholder community with interests in marine resources;
coordinate the United States Federal research and monitoring program with research and monitoring programs and scientists from other nations;

establish or designate, and contribute to as appropriate, an Ocean Acidification Information Exchange to make information on ocean acidification and coastal acidification developed through or utilized by the interagency ocean acidification program accessible through electronic means, including information which would be useful to policymakers, researchers, and other stakeholders in mitigating or adapting to the impacts of ocean acidification and coastal acidification; and1So in original.1

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Chair of the Subcommittee shall establish an Ocean Acidification Advisory Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the biennial report specified in subsection (d)(2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the strategic research plan in subsection (d)(3);

children.label: B
children.text: provide ongoing advice to the Subcommittee and the interagency working group on matters related to Federal activities on ocean and coastal acidification, including impacts and mitigation of ocean and coastal acidification; and

children.label: i
children.text: efforts to coordinate research and monitoring activities related to ocean acidification and coastal acidification; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the best practices for the standards developed for data archiving under .section 3705(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Two representatives of the shellfish, lobster, or crab industry.

children.label: B
children.text: One representative of the finfish industry.

children.label: C
children.text: One representative of seafood processors.

children.label: D
children.text: Three representatives from academia, including both natural and social sciences.

children.label: E
children.text: One representative of recreational fishing.

children.label: F
children.text: One representative of a relevant nongovernmental organization.

children.label: G
children.text: Six representatives from relevant State and local governments with policy or regulatory authorities related to ocean acidification and coastal acidification.

children.label: H
children.text: One representative from the Alaska Ocean Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: I
children.text: One representative from the California Current Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: J
children.text: One representative from the Northeast Coastal Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: K
children.text: One representative from the Southeast Coastal Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: L
children.text: One representative from the Gulf of Mexico Coastal Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: M
children.text: One representative from the Mid-Atlantic Coastal Acidification Network or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical region and has a similar purpose.

children.label: N
children.text: One representative from the Pacific Islands Ocean Observing System or a subsequent entity that represents the island territories and possessions of the United States in the Pacific Ocean, and the State of Hawaii and has a similar purpose.

children.label: O
children.text: One representative from the Caribbean Regional Association for Coastal Ocean Observing or a subsequent entity that represents Puerto Rico and the United States Virgin Islands and has a similar purpose.

children.label: P
children.text: One representative from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Olympic Coast Ocean Acidification Sentinel Site or a subsequent entity that represents the same geographical representation.

children.label: Q
children.text: One representative from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration shall serve as an ex-officio member of the Advisory Board without a vote.

children.label: i
children.text: represent the interest group for which each seat is designated;

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate expertise on ocean acidification or coastal acidification and its scientific, economic, industry, cultural, and community impacts; and

children.label: iii
children.text: have a record of distinguished service with respect to ocean acidification or coastal acidification, and such impacts;

children.label: B
children.text: give consideration to nominations and recommendations from the members of the interagency working group and the public for such appointments; and

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that an appropriate balance of scientific, industry, State and local resource managers, and geographical interests are represented by the members of the Advisory Board.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be appointed for a 5-year term; and

children.label: B
children.text: may be appointed to no more than two terms.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: 6 Chair
children.text: The Chair of the Subcommittee shall appoint one member of the Advisory Board to serve as the Chair of the Advisory Board.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 7 Meetings
children.text: Not less than once each calendar year, the Advisory Board shall meet at such times and places as may be designated by the Chair of the Advisory Board, in consultation with the Chair of the Subcommittee and the Chair of the interagency working group.

children.heading: Briefing
children.label: 8 Briefing
children.text: The Chair of the Advisory Board shall brief the Subcommittee and the interagency working group on the progress of the Advisory Board as necessary or at the request of the Subcommittee.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: i Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) may be construed as affecting any requirement to consult with Indian Tribes under Executive Order 13175 ( note; relating to consultation and coordination with Tribal governments) or any other applicable law or policy.25 U.S.C. 5301

children.heading: Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.label: 10 Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.text: Section 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act shall not apply to the Advisory Board for 10 years from .August 9, 20222See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any Federal agency with a representative serving on the interagency working group established under this section may, either individually or in cooperation with one or more agencies, carry out a program to award prizes competitively under . An agency seeking to carry out such a program shall carry out such program in coordination with the chair of such interagency working group.section 3719 of title 15

children.heading: Purposes
children.label: 2 Purposes
children.text: Any prize competition carried out under this subsection shall be for the purpose of stimulating innovation to advance our Nation’s ability to understand, research, or monitor ocean acidification or its impacts, or to develop management or adaptation options for responding to ocean and coastal acidification.

children.heading: Priority programs
children.label: 3 Priority programs
children.text: Priority shall be given to establishing programs under this section that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the impacts of ocean and coastal acidification.

children.label: A
children.text: includes a summary of federally funded ocean acidification research and monitoring activities, including the budget for each of these activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: describes the progress in developing the plan required under .section 3704 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a summary of federally funded ocean acidification research and monitoring activities, including the budget for each of these activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: an analysis of the progress made toward achieving the goals and priorities for interagency strategic research plan developed by the Subcommittee under .section 3704 of this title

children.heading: Strategic research plan
children.label: 3 Strategic research plan
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Subcommittee shall transmit the strategic research plan developed under to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science and Technology and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives. A revised plan shall be submitted at least once every 5 years until 2031 thereafter.March 30, 2009section 3704 of this title1

children.label: i
children.text: is named the “Ocean Chemistry Coastal Community Vulnerability Assessment”;

children.label: ii
children.text: identifies gaps in ocean acidification monitoring by public, academic, and private assets in the network of regional coastal observing systems;

children.label: iii
children.text: identifies geographic areas which have gaps in ocean acidification research;

children.label: iv
children.text: identifies United States coastal communities, including island communities, fishing communities, low-population rural communities, tribal and subsistence communities, and island communities, that may be impacted by ocean acidification;

children.label: v
children.text: identifies impacts of changing ocean carbonate chemistry on the communities described in clause (iv), including impacts from changes in ocean and coastal marine resources that are not managed by the Federal Government;

children.label: vi
children.text: identifies gaps in understanding of the impacts of ocean acidification on economically or commercially important species, particularly those which support United States commercial, recreational, and tribal fisheries and aquaculture;

children.label: vii
children.text: identifies habitats that may be particularly vulnerable to corrosive sea water, including areas experiencing multiple stressors such as hypoxia, sedimentation, and harmful algal blooms;

children.label: viii
children.text: identifies areas in which existing National Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observation System assets, including unmanned maritime systems, may be leveraged as platforms for the deployment of new sensors or other applicable observing technologies;

children.label: I
children.text: the National Marine Fisheries Service and the Office for Coastal Management of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration;

children.label: II
children.text: regional coastal observing systems established under ;section 3603(c)(4) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: regional ocean acidification networks; and

children.label: IV
children.text: sea grant programs (as defined in ); andsection 1122 of this title

children.label: x
children.text: is written in consultation with experts, including subsistence users, academia, and stakeholders familiar with the economic, social, ecological, geographic, and resource concerns of coastal communities in the United States.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: i Initial report
children.text: The initial report required under subparagraph (A) shall include the information described in clauses (i) through (viii) of that subparagraph on a national level.

children.label: I
children.text: may describe the information described in clauses (i) through (viii) of that subparagraph on a national level; or

children.label: II
children.text: may consist of separate reports for each region of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: Regional reports
children.label: iii Regional reports
children.text: If the Subcommittee opts to prepare a report required under subparagraph (A) as separate regional reports under clause (ii)(II), the Subcommittee shall submit a report for each region of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration not less frequently than once during each 6-year reporting period.

children.heading: Appropriate committees of Congress defined
children.label: C Appropriate committees of Congress defined
children.text: In this paragraph and in paragraph (5), the term “appropriate committees of Congress” means the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.
